description_long: This object is a vintage-style computer monitor with a boxy, angular design reminiscent of early personal computers from the 1980s. The front features a large, slightly recessed rectangular screen framed by a thick bezel, with a single round button and a horizontal slot below, likely representing a power button and a floppy disk drive. The sides are smooth and gently sloped, with a stepped base that supports the monitor and adds visual weight. The rear is flat with a subtle rectangular panel, possibly for access or ventilation, and a grid of small ventilation holes on the lower section. The entire object is rendered in a uniform, light gray color, giving it a utilitarian and retro appearance. The surface appears hard and matte, with minimal decorative elements and a focus on functional geometry.
description: A retro, boxy computer monitor with a thick bezel, single button, and floppy drive slot, all in light gray with a matte finish.
description_view_0: Front view shows the large screen, thick bezel, a round button, and a horizontal slot below the display.
description_view_1: Left side view reveals the sloped back of the monitor and the stepped, ribbed base.
description_view_2: Rear view displays a flat back with a rectangular panel and a grid of ventilation holes on the lower section.
description_view_3: Right side view mirrors the left, showing the sloped back and ribbed base without additional features.
attribute_screen_bezel_design: The bezel is thick, rectangular, and slightly raised, with rounded corners and a uniform light gray plastic appearance. It is plain with no color contrast or decorative elements.
attribute_stand_type: The stand is a wide, stepped flat base integrated with the monitor, featuring ribbed detailing along the sides and front. It is made of the same light gray plastic as the rest of the monitor and does not appear adjustable.
attribute_button_placement: A single round button is located on the lower right of the front panel, below the screen and to the right of the floppy drive slot.
